name: Niall Horan
description:
'''Niall James Horan''' ({{IPAc-en|ˈ|n|aɪ|ə|l|_|ˈ|h|ɔːr|ən}} {{respell|NY|əl|_|HAW|rən}};{{cbignore}} born 13 September 1993) is an Irish singer-songwriter. He rose to prominence as a member of the boy band [[One Direction]], formed in 2010 on the singing competition ''[[The X Factor (British TV series)|The X Factor]]''. The group released five albums and went on to become one of the best-selling [[boy band]]s of all time. 

After the band declared a hiatus in 2016, Horan signed a recording deal as a solo artist with [[Capitol Records]]. He has since released three albums: ''[[Flicker (album)|Flicker]]'' (2017), ''[[Heartbreak Weather]]'' (2020), and ''[[The Show (Niall Horan album)|The Show]]'' (2023). ''Flicker'' debuted at number one in Ireland and the US, and reached the top three in Australia and the UK. The album's first two singles, "[[This Town (Niall Horan song)|This Town]]" and "[[Slow Hands (Niall Horan song)|Slow Hands]]", reached the top 20 in several countries. ''Heartbreak Weather'' was released in March 2020, and debuted at number one in the UK, Ireland, Mexico, and the US. Horan released his third studio album, ''The Show'', in June 2023, which reached number one in seven countries, and spawned two singles—"[[Heaven (Niall Horan song)|Heaven]]" and "[[Meltdown (Niall Horan song)|Meltdown]]".
